client_context.ai_history: Deployed rules-based auto-adjudication for claims. No AI/ML in prior authorization. Exploring Autonomize AI platform for PA automation. CIO has executive mandate for AI-driven operational efficiency.

problem_statement.title: High-Cost, Slow Prior Authorization Processing Due to Manual Review and Fragmented Systems
problem_statement.summary: NCHP processes ~2.5M prior authorization requests per month across 45M covered lives and 20 lines of business. Current PA processing relies on manual review of faxed requests (60% of volume), fragmented clinical data across 12+ EMR systems, and poor integration with their TriZetto Facets core administration platform. Average turnaround is 5 business days, with only 15% auto-approval rate. The CMS Prior Authorization Final Rule (CMS-0057-F) requires FHIR-based PA APIs and faster turnaround by January 1, 2027, creating regulatory urgency.

current_state.process_description: PA requests arrive via fax (60%), proprietary web portal (25%), and X12 278 EDI transactions (15%). Faxed requests are manually triaged by intake specialists who key data into the Facets core system. Clinical reviewers then access 12+ disparate EMR systems to gather clinical evidence, cross-reference against InterQual/MCG guidelines (partially digitized), and render determination. Determinations are communicated back via fax, portal notification, or X12 278 response.
pain_points: 5-day average PA turnaround time — 3x industry best practice
60% of requests arrive via fax requiring manual data entry and OCR
Clinical staff spend 40% of time on PA paperwork instead of clinical review
450 FTEs dedicated to PA processing at ~$65K/FTE fully loaded ($29.25M annual labor)
15% auto-approval rate vs. 50-70% industry benchmark with AI
Fragmented clinical data across 12+ EMR systems with inconsistent FHIR adoption
No unified audit trail — compliance risk for HIPAA and CMS-0057-F
Provider abrasion — 14 hours/week per physician spent on PA (AMA survey)
current_state.annual_cost: $29.25M labor + $4.5M infrastructure = ~$33.75M total PA operational cost

desired_state.description: AI-driven PA processing using Autonomize AI multi-agent platform that automates intake, clinical data aggregation, guidelines matching, and determination for straightforward cases while routing complex cases to clinical reviewers with pre-assembled evidence packages. Fully FHIR-compliant PA APIs meeting CMS-0057-F requirements. Multi-tenant architecture supporting 20 LOBs.
target_improvements: Reduce PA turnaround from 5 days to < 24 hours for auto-determined cases
Increase auto-determination rate from 15% to 60% in Phase 1
Reduce PA FTE count by 40% (180 FTEs) through automation
Achieve CMS-0057-F compliance before January 1, 2027 deadline
Unified audit trail for all PA decisions with full explainability
Single clinical data view aggregating all 12+ EMR sources via FHIR R4
Support all 20 LOBs on multi-tenant platform with configuration-driven rules

ai_suitability_assessment.rationale: Prior authorization is a textbook AI automation use case: high-volume repeatable classification task with structured decision criteria (clinical guidelines), abundant historical training data (30M PA decisions/year), measurable accuracy metrics (approval/denial concordance with clinical review), and clear ROI. Multi-format ingestion (fax OCR, X12 parsing, FHIR standardization) is well-served by multi-agent AI orchestration.
favorable_factors: High volume (2.5M/month) amortizes AI platform cost rapidly
Repeatable classification against codified clinical guidelines
Rich historical decision data for model training and validation
Clear measurable KPIs (turnaround time, auto-determination rate, accuracy)
Autonomize AI PA Copilot already deployed at 3 of top 5 US health plans
Regulatory deadline (CMS-0057-F) creates executive urgency and budget
Human-in-the-loop preserves clinical oversight for complex cases
risk_factors: Auto-denial decisions carry regulatory and legal risk — Phase 1 excludes auto-denial
Fax OCR accuracy for handwritten clinical notes may have lower confidence
Clinical guidelines change quarterly — model must adapt without full retraining
PHI handling requires strict HIPAA compliance across all AI processing stages

pain_points: PROCESS INEFFICIENCY: 5-day PA turnaround due to sequential manual workflow — clinical staff spend 40% of time on paperwork. Impact: $29.25M/year labor. Pattern: multi-agent AI pipeline.
DATA QUALITY: 60% fax-based intake requires manual data entry — high error rate. Impact: 15+ min/fax. Pattern: OCR + NLP ingestion agent.
INTEGRATION GAP: Clinical data fragmented across 12+ EMR systems. Impact: reviewers manually access multiple systems. Pattern: FHIR R4 facade + legacy connectors.
COMPLIANCE RISK: No unified audit trail — CMS-0057-F deadline Jan 2027. Impact: regulatory non-compliance. Pattern: immutable audit log + FHIR PA API.
SCALABILITY LIMIT: Manual process cannot scale to 20 LOBs. Impact: each LOB requires more FTEs. Pattern: multi-tenant config-driven rules engine.
KNOWLEDGE LOSS: Review expertise concentrated in senior staff. Impact: inconsistent determinations. Pattern: AI decision support + feedback loop.

stakeholders.name: Dr. Sarah Chen
stakeholders.communication_preference: Clinical outcome metrics, patient impact, weekly clinical review

stakeholders.name: Marcus Williams
stakeholders.communication_preference: Operational KPIs, FTE impact, cost savings, bi-weekly status

stakeholders.name: Priya Patel
stakeholders.communication_preference: Architecture diagrams, integration timelines, risk registers

stakeholders.name: James Morrison
stakeholders.communication_preference: Compliance mapping, audit trail specs, regulatory deadlines

functional_requirements.title: Multi-format PA request ingestion
functional_requirements.description: Securely ingest PA requests from fax (OCR), web portal (API), and X12 278 EDI. Convert all formats into unified structured format for AI processing.
acceptance_criteria: Fax OCR >95% character accuracy on printed text
X12 278 parsed within 5 seconds
Portal API validates PA requests in real-time
All formats converge to common PA request data model

functional_requirements.title: Clinical data aggregation and standardization
functional_requirements.description: Securely access clinical data from FHIR R4 endpoints and legacy databases. Standardize into unified clinical view per patient. Support unstructured notes, lab results, patient history.
acceptance_criteria: FHIR R4 client retrieves patient data from compliant endpoints
Legacy connectors access clinical data via secure APIs
NLP extracts entities from unstructured clinical notes
Unified clinical view assembled within 30 seconds

functional_requirements.title: AI-driven clinical review and determination
functional_requirements.description: Compare PA clinical evidence against InterQual/MCG guidelines using Autonomize AI multi-agent orchestration. Produce determination with confidence score and rationale.
acceptance_criteria: >=95% concordance with clinical reviewer decisions
Confidence score for every determination
Cases below 0.85 confidence routed to human reviewer
Clinical rationale with specific guideline citations
60% auto-determination within 6 months of go-live

functional_requirements.title: Member eligibility verification
functional_requirements.description: Real-time eligibility check against Facets before PA determination. Verify enrollment, benefit coverage, and contract rules.
acceptance_criteria: Eligibility check within 3 seconds
Covers enrollment, benefit plan, service-specific coverage
Ineligible requests rejected with reason code

functional_requirements.title: Human-in-the-loop clinical review workflow
functional_requirements.description: Route low-confidence cases to reviewers with pre-assembled evidence packages. Capture reviewer corrections for model improvement.
acceptance_criteria: Complete evidence package — no additional system access needed
Reviewer can approve, deny, or request more info
Corrections captured for feedback loop
Reviewer decision time reduced 50%

functional_requirements.title: PA determination response delivery
functional_requirements.description: Deliver determination back to originating system — fax, portal, or X12 278 — with rationale and conditions.
acceptance_criteria: Response in originating channel within 1 hour of determination
X12 278 response ASC X12 compliant
Portal includes determination, rationale, appeal instructions

functional_requirements.title: Comprehensive audit trail
functional_requirements.description: Immutable audit log for every PA decision with full traceability — decision maker, data considered, guidelines applied, confidence, overrides.
acceptance_criteria: Complete audit record within 5 seconds of determination
Immutable and tamper-evident records
Data lineage: source docs, clinical data, guidelines
Supports regulatory inquiry with full reconstruction
7-year retention per HIPAA

functional_requirements.title: FHIR R4 Prior Authorization API (CMS-0057-F)
functional_requirements.description: FHIR R4 PA API per HL7 Da Vinci PAS Implementation Guide, meeting CMS-0057-F requirements.
acceptance_criteria: FHIR R4 endpoint per Da Vinci PAS IG
Real-time PA submission and status inquiry
Specific denial reason in response per CMS-0057-F
Compliant by January 1, 2027

functional_requirements.title: Multi-LOB configuration engine
functional_requirements.description: Configuration-driven rules engine for 20 LOBs with LOB-specific rules, criteria, and workflows — without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: New LOB onboarded via config in < 2 weeks
LOB rules isolated — no cross-LOB impact
Supports LOB-specific guidelines, thresholds, workflows
Config version-controlled with rollback

functional_requirements.title: MLOps monitoring and model lifecycle
functional_requirements.description: Production monitoring with drift detection, alerting, and feedback loop from reviewer corrections to model improvement.
acceptance_criteria: Daily statistical drift detection (PSI, KS test)
Weekly outcome monitoring (overturn rate, appeal rate)
Automated drift alerts
Daily correction aggregation, weekly evaluation, monthly fine-tuning
A/B testing before production deployment

functional_requirements.title: Operational dashboards and reporting
functional_requirements.description: Real-time dashboards: volume, turnaround, auto-determination rate, accuracy, queue depth, per-LOB breakdowns.
acceptance_criteria: Executive dashboard: turnaround, cost savings, auto-determination rate
Operations dashboard: queue depth, workload, SLA compliance
Clinical dashboard: accuracy, guideline match, appeal rates
Per-LOB filtering

functional_requirements.title: Provider self-service PA status portal
functional_requirements.description: Real-time PA status inquiry for providers with secure authentication.
acceptance_criteria: Real-time status check via web portal
Secure provider authentication
Upload additional clinical documentation

sources.name: TriZetto Facets Core System
sources.quality_notes: Authoritative for enrollment, benefits, contract rules.

sources.name: Fax Server / Inbound Fax Queue
sources.quality_notes: Variable quality — printed forms readable, handwritten requires specialized OCR.

sources.name: FHIR R4 Clinical Data Endpoints
sources.volume: Queried per PA for clinical evidence
sources.quality_notes: Newer EMR systems. ~40% of clinical data.

sources.name: Legacy Clinical Databases
sources.quality_notes: Inconsistent schemas. Some HL7 v2 compliant.

constraints.budget_range: Enterprise-scale — justified by $33.75M annual PA cost and CMS-0057-F mandate
constraints.timeline_note: 12-week roadmap: Discovery, Architecture Sign-Off, Integration Build, QA/UAT, Go-Live
technology_restrictions: Must use Autonomize AI Genesis Platform for AI orchestration
Payer AWS infrastructure for integration and data platform
All PHI processing within payer VPC (HIPAA)
constraints.team_constraints: Autonomize AI: platform + AI engineering. Payer: integration devs, clinical SMEs, compliance. External SI may augment.

success_criteria.description: Percentage of PA requests auto-determined by AI without human review
Average hours from receipt to determination for auto-determined cases
Concordance with clinical reviewer on blinded sample
PA processing FTE reduction through automation
CMS PA Final Rule FHIR API requirements met
PA decisions with complete audit trail

in_scope: PA request ingestion from fax, web portal, X12 278 EDI
Clinical data aggregation from FHIR R4 and legacy sources
AI-driven clinical review and determination via Autonomize AI
FHIR R4 PA API per CMS-0057-F / Da Vinci PAS
MLOps monitoring with drift detection and feedback loop
out_of_scope: Member enrollment and benefits administration (read-only Facets access)
scope_boundaries.justification: Scope bounded to PA lifecycle from ingestion through determination. Adjacent systems accessed read-only. Implementation excluded per SA scope boundary.

assumptions: Client is a large US health plan (~45M members, 20 LOBs)
Autonomize AI Genesis Platform and PA Copilot are production-ready, HIPAA-certified
Payer has existing AWS infrastructure with VPC and IAM
TriZetto Facets APIs available and documented
InterQual/MCG clinical guidelines licensed and digitized
40%+ clinical data sources support FHIR R4; rest via legacy connectors
12-week timeline for initial production with first LOB (Commercial)
Additional LOBs onboarded post-go-live via configuration
Clinical SMEs available 50% during integration build
Existing HIPAA controls extend to new solution
Auto-denial excluded from Phase 1 — auto-approval only
